# MAMEUIx v0.1.6

MAMEUIx v0.1.6 adds an opt-in redesigned interface and a Software Lists browser preview. The existing dock-panel interface remains the default.

## Highlights

### Redesigned UI preview

- Adds experimental Library, game detail, ROM verification, and settings views with responsive layouts and Public Sans typography
- Can be enabled under **Preferences → UI shell → Redesign preview (experimental)** or at startup with `mameuix --redesign`
- Makes `Ctrl`/`Cmd`+`F` focus search, separates missing ROM sets from CHD/ROM and driver issues, and makes long settings pages scrollable
- Preserves the redesign palette when legacy theme settings change
- Replaces misleading shader Apply controls with read-only BGFX status and explains why bundled GLSL effect names cannot be used as MAME BGFX chain names

You can switch back to **Dock panels (default)** at any time.

![Redesigned Library](https://github.com/firesand/MAMEUIx/blob/v0.1.6/screenshot/Redesign_Library.png?raw=1)

### Software Lists preview

- Reads MAME software-list hash XML files from a configured file or directory
- Provides list and text filters for software name, description, publisher, year, and list
- Checks best-effort media-path presence from configured software-list ROM roots without claiming a ROM audit; archive and CHD contents are not inspected
- Rejects ROM-manager DAT XML with a clear error instead of treating it as MAME hash data

This preview is for browsing and inspecting software-list metadata. Path status is only a filesystem hint and may not represent merged layouts; launching software-list entries is not implemented in this release.

### Platform and documentation updates

- Simplifies the About dialog to stable project information instead of a hard-coded version and feature inventory
- Moves public installation, MAME folder-layout, and BGFX/GLSL guides under `docs/`
- Adds initial FreeBSD amd64 path discovery and source-build instructions; FreeBSD remains unverified and is not an officially supported platform
- Builds the release AppImage on Ubuntu 22.04 for compatibility with glibc 2.35 and newer

## AppImage

MAME is a separate runtime dependency and is not bundled. Install MAME first, then run the x86_64 AppImage:

```bash
chmod +x MAMEUIx-0.1.6-x86_64.AppImage
./MAMEUIx-0.1.6-x86_64.AppImage
```

Launch directly into the opt-in preview with:

```bash
./MAMEUIx-0.1.6-x86_64.AppImage --redesign
```

If FUSE is unavailable:

```bash
APPIMAGE_EXTRACT_AND_RUN=1 ./MAMEUIx-0.1.6-x86_64.AppImage
```

See the [installation guide](https://github.com/firesand/MAMEUIx/blob/main/docs/INSTALL.md) for source builds and native Linux packages.
The release also includes `MAMEUIx-0.1.6-x86_64.AppImage.sha256` for checksum verification.

## Requirements

- Linux x86_64 for the published AppImage
- MAME 0.200 or newer
- Rust 1.85 or newer when building from source
